Fix: Investigate the deadlock, fix the underlying issue, and re-enable the test.","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-16T18:17:22.103838-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T01:21:01.952723-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b3og","depends_on_id":"bd-tggf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T18:19:05.740642-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}],"deleted_at":"2025-12-25T01:21:01.952723-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"bug"}
 {"id":"bd-b6xo","title":"Remove or fix ClearDirtyIssues() - race condition risk (bd-52)","description":"Code health review found internal/storage/sqlite/dirty.go still exposes old ClearDirtyIssues() method (lines 103-108) which clears ALL dirty issues without checking what was actually exported.

Data loss risk: If export fails after some issues written to JSONL but before ClearDirtyIssues called, changes to remaining dirty issues will be lost.

The safer ClearDirtyIssuesByID() (lines 113-132) exists and clears only exported issues.

Fix: Either remove old method or mark it deprecated and ensure no code paths use it.","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-16T18:17:20.534625-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T01:21:01.952723-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b6xo","depends_on_id":"bd-tggf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T18:19:05.633738-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}],"deleted_at":"2025-12-25T01:21:01.952723-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"bug"}

 {"id":"bd-qe7j","title":"Code smell: Incomplete TODOs in formula/types.go","description":"internal/formula/types.go has multiple TODO items for features that are defined in the schema but not implemented:

- Line 170: Expand field not implemented in bd cook
- Line 174: ExpandVars field not implemented
- Line 179: Gate integration pending (bd-udsi gates)
- Line 186: Condition evaluation not implemented
- Line 210: Additional expansion features

**Problem:**
- Schema defines features that aren't implemented
- Can confuse users who try to use these fields
- Creates maintenance burden of unused code paths

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Either implement the features OR
- [ ] Remove unimplemented fields from schema OR
- [ ] Document clearly which fields are future-only
- [ ] Update related beads issues if they exist","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2025-12-28T18:59:59.460434-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/dave","updated_at":"2025-12-30T15:44:43.358286-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T00:39:56.504522-08:00","close_reason":"Merged via refinery","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qe7j","depends_on_id":"bd-ox1o","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-28T19:00:23.747872-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-qioh","title":"Standardize error handling: replace direct fmt.Fprintf+os.Exit with FatalError","description":"Standardize error handling in cmd/bd/ using FatalError pattern.

## Current State
~200+ instances of direct `fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, ...) + os.Exit(1)` pattern scattered across cmd/bd/*.go files.

## Target Pattern

Use existing FatalError helper (or create if missing):

```go
// In cmd/bd/helpers.go or similar
func FatalError(format string, args ...interface{}) {
    fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Error: "+format+"\n", args...)
    os.Exit(1)
}

func FatalErrorf(err error, context string) {
    fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Error: %s: %v\n", context, err)
    os.Exit(1)
}
```

## Transformation

```go
// Before
fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Error: %v\n", err)
os.Exit(1)

// After
FatalError("%v", err)

// Before
fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Error: invalid --since duration: %v\n", err)
os.Exit(1)

// After
FatalError("invalid --since duration: %v", err)
```

## Files to Update (by occurrence count)
Run: `grep -c "os.Exit(1)" cmd/bd/*.go | sort -t: -k2 -rn | head -20`

Priority files (highest occurrence):
- close.go
- show.go
- sync.go
- init.go
- list.go
- create.go
- update.go

## Implementation Steps

1. **Check if FatalError exists** in cmd/bd/helpers.go or create it
2. **Create migration script** or use sed:
   ```bash
   # Find patterns to replace
   grep -rn "fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr.*Error.*\n.*os.Exit(1)" cmd/bd/
   ```
3. **Replace systematically** file by file
4. **Run tests** after each file to verify behavior unchanged
5. **Run linter** to catch any missed patterns

## Verification
```bash
# Count remaining direct exits (should be near zero)
grep -c "os.Exit(1)" cmd/bd/*.go | awk -F: "{sum+=\$2} END {print sum}"

# Run tests
go test -short ./cmd/bd/...
```

## Success Criteria
- All error exits use FatalError/FatalErrorf
- Consistent "Error: " prefix on all error messages
- Tests pass
- No behavior changes (exit codes remain 1)","notes":"## Progress (Dec 2025)

Standardized error handling in 3 major files:
- compact.go: All 48 os.Exit(1) calls converted to FatalError
- sync.go: All error patterns converted (kept 1 valid summary exit)
- migrate.go: 4 patterns converted

## Remaining Work
~326 fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Error:") patterns remain across ~30 files.
High-count files remaining: show.go (16), dep.go (14), gate.go (28), init.go (11).

## Verification
- Build compiles successfully
- Tests pass","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T18:17:19.309394-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T14:14:37.939802-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T14:14:37.939802-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qioh","depends_on_id":"bd-iz5t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T12:44:07.43514-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
